summary: Laid‑back Paris show with soaring Eyes Of The World and strong Seastones
blurb: The set features standout Peggy‑O, an extended Deal intro and a soaring Eyes Of The World. Phil’s bass is consistently praised while Bob’s rhythm guitar is largely buried in the mix. Seastones adds an experimental edge to the second set.
review: On September 21, 1974 at Paris’s Palais Des Sports the Dead delivered a relaxed yet adventurous performance that still rewards repeated spins. The first set rolls through familiar terrain – Bertha, Sugaree and a crisp Peggy‑O – before slipping into an extended, funk‑laden Deal intro that sets up a soaring Eyes Of The World segue into China Doll. After a brief intermission the second set dives into experimental territory with Seastones*, then bursts back into the classic double‑Playin’ In The Band jam, while Phil’s “smoking” bass drives every groove. The third set supplies fan‑favorites – Uncle John’s Band, Ship Of Fools and an emotionally charged Morning Dew – before closing with Sugar Magnolia and a rousing Casey Jones encore. Across all three soundboard sources the band is in solid form: Phil Lesh’s bass work shines, Jerry Garcia’s lead lines are tasteful (if occasionally restrained), and Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann keep a steady pulse, even if Mickey’s playing is described as pushing ahead at times. The main drawback is the mix – Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar is often buried or missing, and the panning can feel odd – but the musical moments more than compensate for these technical quirks.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise standout songs (Peggy-O, Eyes Of The World, Seastones) and Phil's bass, while only a single source notes mixed/negative aspects (Jerry slow, Bob buried). Overall sentiment is clearly positive, warranting a moderate +1.1 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard with good stereo imaging captures the highlights clearly, despite the occasional Bob mix issue.

key_highlights: Peggy‑O and Deal deliver a relaxed yet funky first‑set momentum.
Eyes Of The World soars with expansive improvisation.
Seastones* provides an experimental interlude that shifts the show’s texture.
Phil Lesh’s bass is consistently described as ‘smoking’ and drives the groove.

band_performance.Jerry: Provides solid lead work and tasteful solos; some listeners note a slower, more restrained approach on this night.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers ‘smoking’ bass throughout, praised for both groove and vocal contribution on Uncle John's Band.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar is largely buried or missing in the mix, especially noticeable early in the set.
band_performance.Keith: Atmospheric keyboards add depth to Seastones*; balance issues are noted but overall texture is appreciated.
band_performance.Mickey: Steady drumming with occasional over‑playing that pushes ahead of the beat, yet keeps a solid foundation.
